title: Spot Instance Interruption Rate
spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" earliest=-30d
| search eventName="SpotInstanceInterruption"
| stats count as intr
| append [ search index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
  | search lineItem_UsageType="*SpotUsage*"
  | stats sum(lineItem_NormalizedUsageAmount) as instance_hours ]
| stats sum(intr) as interruptions sum(instance_hours) as ih
| eval intr_per_1k=round(1000*interruptions/nullif(ih,0),2)
description: Interruptions per 1,000 instance-hours by pool and AZ — extends raw event counts (UC-20.1.7) with a **rate** for SLO tracking.
value: Interruptions per 1,000 instance-hours by pool and AZ — extends raw event counts (UC-20.1.7) with a **rate** for SLO tracking.
implementation: Align instance-hour denominator from CUR `NormalizedUsageAmount`. Alert when `intr_per_1k` exceeds baseline for stateful tiers.
visualization: Single value (interruptions per 1k hours), Line chart (rate trend), Table (by AZ).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk Add-on for AWS`, CloudTrail.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `aws:cloudtrail` Spot events, instance-hours from CUR.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Align instance-hour denominator from CUR `NormalizedUsageAmount`. Alert when `intr_per_1k` exceeds baseline for stateful tiers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" earliest=-30d
| search eventName="SpotInstanceInterruption"
| stats count as intr
| append [ search index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
  | search lineItem_UsageType="*SpotUsage*"
  | stats sum(lineItem_NormalizedUsageAmount) as instance_hours ]
| stats sum(intr) as interruptions sum(instance_hours) as ih
| eval intr_per_1k=round(1000*interruptions/nullif(ih,0),2)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Spot Instance Interruption Rate** — Interruptions per 1,000 instance-hours by pool and AZ — extends raw event counts (UC-20.1.7) with a **rate** for SLO tracking.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:cloudtrail` Spot events, instance-hours from CUR. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk Add-on for AWS`, CloudTrail. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=aws, sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **intr_per_1k** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (interruptions per 1k hours), Line chart (rate trend), Table (by AZ).
grandmaExplanation: Cost and capacity tools watch how much we spend on cloud and the data center, and where we have spare or strained capacity.
knownFalsePositives: Spikes from autoscaling events, new workload deployments, batch jobs, or DR drill resources.
